news.news: Wave Life Sciences announced positive data from the RestorAATion-2 trial for WVE-006, an RNA editing oligonucleotide (AIMer) for alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency. The treatment achieved a protective MZ-like phenotype by generating wild-type M-AAT and reducing harmful Z-AAT, with effects sustained for at least three months following dosing.

The company reported that regulatory feedback on a potential accelerated approval pathway for WVE-006 is expected in mid-2026. Additionally, the clinical trial application (CTA) for WVE-008, a GalNAc-conjugated AIMer for PNPLA3 I148M liver disease, is on track for submission in 2026.
